planets.name: Good
planets.summary: Archive cluster around good: 4894 posts; 4 quote-backed receipts pinned.

artifacts.title: Good receipt from 2023-07-21
artifacts.excerpt:
Over a decade ago, I used to bookmark whenever anyone on Reddit accidentally revealed their real first name, in like a screenshot or something.

I'd then wait several years, and randomly check those profiles. Whenever they told a personal story, I'd reply with "Greg!?"

artifacts.title: Think receipt from 2025-07-31
artifacts.excerpt:
@goblinodds I think amazon has a fairly unique threat vector:

Basically anyone can sign up to sell products, and fulfilled by amazon products all get comingled.

So, if you can get Amazon to accept your product, you can launder it.

Big stores buy from wholesalers / manufacturers.

I think he’s still one of the highest quality reviewers for consumer tech, and then occasionally does a weird money-related thing that’s best to ignore. Sigh.

artifacts.title: Way receipt from 2024-01-31
artifacts.excerpt:
@benlandautaylor Agreed, surprised it took so long. It's design is to find annotations that appeal to people who typically disagree, and making fun of institutions like this (in this way) probably appeals to ~everyone.

Needs some Wikipedia-moderator energy around the core use case.

artifacts.title: Way receipt from 2020-12-09
artifacts.excerpt:
My guess on how some communities do this so well:

Over index early during community building to create the vibe you want. ex: Aggressively select for low ego, then act like this is the way things *are*.

Soon, it’s self-perpetuating, because people emulate their surroundings. https://t.co/ofQto7fFO8 https://t.co/zVRfE7MT2d

This year, she’s run Grand Canyon rim to rim and many long runs. 14ers, big climbs/descents, stacking distance. https://t.co/NKjf2YuCp2

artifacts.title: Many receipt from 2020-03-08
artifacts.excerpt:
@ATabarrok Increase. Net demand represents cohorts of separate demand curves. Many wouldn’t fly for any price. Lowering prices has minimal increase in demand from people who are price sensitive, and makes less from price insensitive passengers.

Focus on the 1/3 of the flight that need it.
